prompt: The method reference resolves to the wrong overload. What is the bug?
choices: Method references cannot reference overloaded methods
Consumer<String> expects a method taking String; both print(String) and print(Object) match; the compiler selects the most specific (print(String)) — this is actually unambiguous
The Consumer must be typed as Consumer<Object> to use print(Object)
AmbiguousRef::print is always ambiguous and causes a compile error
explanation: Java overload resolution picks the most specific applicable method. Consumer<String> needs a method accepting String; print(String) is more specific than print(Object), so print(String) is selected. This compiles and prints 'String: hello'. The code is actually correct — the 'bug' is the misconception that it's ambiguous.
